input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule EV.5.2.2 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: ev.5.2.2
            TITLE: if the accumulator container is made from an electrically conductive material:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.5.2.2: If the Accumulator Container is made from an electrically conductive material:
EV.5.2.2 If the Accumulator Container is made from an electrically conductive material: 
a. The poles of the Accumulator Segment(s) and/or cells must be isolated from the inner 
wall of the Accumulator Container with an insulating material that is rated for the 
maximum Tractive System voltage.    
b. All conductive surfaces on the outside of the Accumulator Container must have a low 
resistance connection to the GLV System Ground, see EV.6.7 
c. Any conductive penetrations, such as mounting hardware, must be protected against 
puncturing the insulating barrier.

final_answer: If the Accumulator Container is made from an electrically conductive material: a. The poles of the Accumulator Segment(s) and/or cells must be isolated from the inner wall of the Accumulator Container with an insulating material that is rated for the maximum Tractive System voltage. b. All conductive surfaces on the outside of the Accumulator Container must have a low resistance connection to the GLV System Ground, see EV.6.7 c. Any conductive penetrations, such as mounting hardware, must be protected against puncturing the insulating barrier.
